$comment: Static policy encoding which host→client payload paths are catalog-gated (id-agnostic — future harnesses never touch this file). Not a per-change review mechanism. Method/path support globs (* one segment, ** any depth; method * is non-dot). Catalog methods agent.gui.listHarnesses / agent.list / providers.list must never appear here for host→client payloads — growth on those requires freezing the shipped line and opening a new major with downgrade bridges (amp 003d7586 / devin 407d110 template).

exceptions.reason: Plan source harnessId is gated by which harnesses the host actually lists; old clients only open getPlan for harnesses they already know.
Commands are scoped to a harness the client already selected from a negotiated catalog; additive harness ids on this echo field stay behind that gate.
Models are scoped to a harness the client already selected from a negotiated catalog; additive harness ids on this echo field stay behind that gate.
Models are scoped to a harness the client already selected; additive harness ids on this echo field stay behind that gate.
Rate-limit rows are only returned for providers the client has already configured; additive provider ids stay behind that gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
awaitLogin echoes the single providerId the client named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Chat stream frames only carry harness ids for chats the client already opened against a negotiated harness catalog; additive ids stay behind that gate.
Action-ack kinds grow with gated client actions; old clients only observe acks for actions they can issue.
Trigger kinds grow with gated product surfaces; old clients only observe kinds their client version can raise.

Parsed-stream MCP identity on autonomous-resume triggers, nullable/.default(null): null renders the plain command trigger card - exactly what a pre-mcp host produces, and old clients strip the key on parse. Deliberately an additive field instead of a new `kind` enum value so an older host's whole-chat safeParse of persisted triggers survives (the wakeTriggers constraint); scoped by field path.
Runtime event union grows with gated harness/event features; old clients only subscribe to chats whose harnesses they can open.
Per-harness message anchor variants grow with gated harnesses; old clients only open those harnesses from a negotiated catalog.
Per-harness session-anchor variants grow with gated harnesses; old clients only open those harnesses from a negotiated catalog.
Content-block union variants grow with gated product surfaces; old clients only render blocks their version knows for chats they can open.

Persistence-mirror profile field on a parsed stream: the chat stream client parses every serverFrame through the live schema, and the field is nullable/.default(null) where null IS the pre-profile semantic (ambient/legacy session) - an old 1.3 host's frames materialize exactly what a pre-profile session genuinely has. Old 1.3 clients parse with plain (stripping) objects, so a new host's frames degrade cleanly. Streams have no per-frame version transforms, so a minor bump would change nothing at runtime; the exception is scoped by field path, so unrelated additions stay guarded.

Persistence-mirror profile field on a parsed stream (see the **.profileId entry for the full rationale): nullable/.default(null), null is the pre-profile semantic, frames are live-schema-parsed both directions; scoped by field path.

Persistence-mirror profile field on a parsed stream (see the **.profileId entry for the full rationale): nullable/.default(null), null is the pre-profile semantic, frames are live-schema-parsed both directions; scoped by field path.

Persistence-mirror profile field on a parsed stream (see the **.profileId entry for the full rationale): nullable/.default(null), null is the pre-profile semantic, the GUI additionally falls back to its own palette hash when null; scoped by field path.

Session-anchor/chain seed-coverage field on a parsed stream (frames are live-schema-parsed, so the tolerance executes): nullable/.default(null), and null is the documented 'no seed context ever existed' semantic - literally true for a host that predates fake-context seeding (#187). No shipped client reads the field; only the host's own seeding consumes it.
Parsed-stream field with .default([]): an empty wake list is exactly what a host that predates ScheduleWakeup (#196) has. No shipped client consumes the field outside tests.

Parsed-stream field with .default([]) whose decode codec merges entries into the block's pre-existing `triggers` list - an empty merge is a byte-for-byte no-op, so old-host autonomous_resume blocks render exactly as they did before #205.

Parsed-stream field, nullable/.default(null): null renders the plain subagent card, which is the only card a pre-workflow (#235) host can produce - old hosts cannot run workflow fleets, so no data exists to lose.

Parsed-stream field, nullable/.default(null): the text block's `text` always carries the full plain rendering and the client falls back to it when the notice is null - the documented pre-#243 behavior.

Parsed-stream flag with .default(false) on tool_call/subagent blocks: false renders the plain error end-state badge, exactly the shipped pre-#152 treatment (a legacy error-string fallback for old blocks is retained in the tool segment).

Steer-block provenance, nullable/.default(null). The steered USER row remains the primary record of who sent a steered message and is unchanged; this only mirrors its sender onto the block so the renderer's ORPHAN fallback (block present, user row missing) can tell an agent-to-agent steer from a human one instead of rendering it as user-authored text. null is exactly what a pre-field host produces, and the fallback then renders the plain user row it always did - so an old host's frames degrade to the shipped behavior, and an old client strips the key and is unaffected. Scoped by field path.

The steer.submitted counterpart of the **.blocks.items.anyOf[*].properties.sender entry (nullable/.default(null)): the shared accumulator copies it onto the steer block it builds, so host and client derive an identical block. Absent from a pre-field host's events, which yields the same null the block schema defaults to.

Parsed-stream background-task marker (block: nullable/.default(false); events: .optional() with `?? null` in the shared accumulator): falsy means no background-card promotion, so old-host tool calls collapse into the generic activity group exactly as before #127.
Parsed-stream background output (block: nullable/.default(null); events: .optional() with merge-preserve in the accumulator): null renders no output section, the pre-#127 behavior.
Parsed-stream event timestamp, .optional(): the accumulator falls back to the foreground spawn time or renders no duration - the pre-#127 timer behavior.

Parsed-stream block timestamp, nullable/.default(null): consumers anchor timing on the block's always-present `timestamp` when null and deliberately render no duration instead of a misleading 0s - the pre-#127 behavior.
Parsed-stream event timestamp, .optional(): the accumulator substitutes `event.timestamp` (the pre-#127 anchor) when absent.
Parsed-stream block timestamp, nullable/.default(null): null renders no duration, matching pre-#127 tool segments.

Parsed-stream subagent.completed classifier with .default("completed"): the accumulator maps it to status completed/stopped false, which the code documents as reproducing the shipped pre-#152 behavior exactly.
Parsed-stream tool_call.errored classifier with .default("error"): maps to stopped:false, the plain error render every pre-#152 host produced.

Parsed-stream authoritative turn bit, deliberately .optional() with NO default: the store keeps it undefined across frames (`?? state.turnInProgress`) and the session-state resolver then falls back to the layer-2 local approximation - the exact shipped pre-#165 logic. A default would be wrong here; absence means 'capability missing, approximate'.

Parsed-stream worktree-binding field, .optional() by design: the schema doc states absence distinguishes an old/empty binding that inherits the Epic's folders from an explicit folderless one (#227), and consumers only strict-compare against "folderless" - undefined takes the inherit branch, which is the only state a pre-folderless host can be in.

Parsed-stream worktree-binding entry field, .optional() with an in-schema note that it must never become a required-field wire break (#228): no shipped client reads it (the merge rollup consumes worktree.listAllForHost facts instead), so absence is inert.

Parsed-stream capability sentinel, .optional() with fail-safe polarity: absent reads as not-supported, so the client never emits ack frames - byte-for-byte the pre-#179 wire behavior, and the safe direction (a pre-ack host cannot even parse an ack client frame). Re-evaluated on every snapshot, so it cannot go stale across reconnects.

Parsed-stream display metadata, nullable/.optional() (in-schema: 'Optional so clients remain compatible with already-shipped hosts', #217): every consumer normalizes to null = idle/unknown and falls back to the derived terminal title - the pre-feature UI.

Parsed-stream exit classifier, nullable/.optional() (in-schema: absent is equivalent to process-exit, the only pre-field behavior a client could assume, #192): consumers collapse null to exitCode-based classification, the exact pre-feature semantics.

Parsed-stream aggregate list, .optional(): the stream client coerces `?? []` and the store falls back to the required singular `epic` aggregate - the pre-#247 per-epic projection. The global monitor is legitimately empty against a host that has no global scope.

Shared terminal-session shape on create/list responses, nullable/.optional() (#217). Same-version unary responses are delivered by cast (the transport only schema-parses when an upgrade chain runs), so absence reaches the store raw - verified safe: the session store and title helper normalize undefined to null = idle/unknown, the pre-feature display.

Shared terminal-session shape on create/list responses, nullable/.optional() (#192). Delivered by cast at same-version; verified safe: the store normalizes `?? null` and every consumer falls back to exitCode-based crash/exit classification - the documented 'absent equals process-exit' pre-field semantic.

Worktree-binding field, .optional() by design (#227): the schema doc defines absence as 'inherit the Epic's folders', the only state a pre-folderless host can produce. Delivered by cast at same-version; verified safe: consumers only strict-compare against "folderless", so undefined takes the inherit branch.

Worktree-binding entry field, .optional() with an in-schema no-wire-break note (#228). Delivered by cast at same-version; verified safe: no shipped client reads it (the merge rollup consumes worktree.listAllForHost facts instead).
Same worktree-binding shape as the worktree.* responses (see that **.workspaceMode entry): absence is the designed 'inherit' state and consumers strict-compare against "folderless".
Same worktree-binding shape as the worktree.* responses (see that **.ownedSubmodules entry): no shipped client reads the field.

TUI harness row flag with .default(true) (#178): default matches the pre-feature reading that every row an old host lists is usable. The method has no caller in any shipped client (host-internal bootstrap only), so the tolerated key is inert on the wire today.

TUI harness row flag with .catch(false), added with an in-schema note that it tolerates old host builds that omit the field (#147): false = probe settled, the pre-feature state. No caller in any shipped client.

Model-row sunset copy, nullable/.optional() with an in-schema note that absent and null are treated identically downstream (#176). Delivered by cast at same-version; verified safe: consumers normalize `?? null` and render no deprecation badge - exactly 'not deprecated', the pre-feature state.
